reasoning: To reach a rating at least 1% higher than 2754 (his rating as of mid-October 2025), Wei Yi would need to gain ~28 Elo points, reaching ~2782. Gaining 28 points at the 2750+ level is an exceptionally rare feat that requires a historic winning streak in classical events, usually over many months. According to recent news, Wei Yi has been fairly inactive (referred to as a "casual" player), finished dead last in Norway Chess 2025, and explicitly declined his qualification spot for the most important end-of-year classical event (the FIDE Grand Swiss 2025). Furthermore, FIDE ratings on Wikipedia update only on a monthly basis based on the FIDE official lists (published on the 1st of each month). Therefore, his rating on November 2 and November 25 will almost certainly reflect his November 1 list rating, which we confidently know won't show a 28-point bump since he hasn't been active in October. By January 2026, he would have had slightly more time, but given his lack of participation in top classical events and the immense difficulty of gaining 28 points, the chance remains extremely low. I am setting the probabilities to near the minimum bound.
